accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and the IHRA/State Department context is factually correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the F/M visa and IHRA context are accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose to revoke/deny student visas for antisemitic conduct.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes the amendment to Section 214 of the Immigration and Nationality Act adding new nonimmigrant status provisions.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures the key conditions: prohibited conduct plus the serious adverse foreign policy consequences determination.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the provisions apply to conduct on or after the date of enactment.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers both the visa revocation/denial mandate and the INA Section 214 amendment, including definitions.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

concise.why: Appropriately detailed without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the Secretary of State, IHRA, State Department, and Section 214 of the INA.

correct_figures.why: The May 26, 2016 IHRA date and F/M visa references are accurately stated.
